title: Transformation Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"elmv0","text":"Quick Ligation products may be transformed by many different methods. The following protocol is recommended by New England Biolabs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"dhudr","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Chill approximately 5 ng, ligation mixture ( 2 µL ) in a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.
Add 50 µL, competent cells to the DNA.
Mix gently by pipetting up and down or flicking the tube 4–5 times to mix the cells and DNA. Do not vortex.
Place the mixture On ice for 00:30:00 . Do not mix.

Heat shock at 42 °C for 00:00:30 . Do not mix. Please note: For the duration and temperature of the heat shock step as well as for the media to be used during the recovery period, please follow the recommendations provided by the competent cells’ manufacturer.
Add 950 µL, room temperature media to the tube.
Place tube at 37 °C for 01:00:00 . Shake vigorously ( 250 rpm, °C, ) or rotate.
Spread 50 µL – 100 µL, cells and ligation mixture onto the plates.
creator: New England   Biolabs
